label: gpt-5.4-mini|low|darkelf|archivist
output_md:
## skill_boundary_brief.md
Define `api_contract_drift_check` as a reusable Soulforge skill for public-safe API contract drift packets. It reviews claimed OpenAPI, TypeScript, and route-handler changes, separates confirmed evidence from assumptions, and produces a bounded remediation plan. The skill must treat real specs, production logs, credentials, incident details, endpoint names tied to private systems, and `_workspaces` runtime files as runtime inputs only.

## skill_package_draft.md
Package: `.registry/skills/api_contract_drift_check/skill.yaml`, `README.md`, optional `codex/SKILL.md`, plus public-safe templates or checklists. The skill should accept redacted packet inputs with fields like `openapi_changed_endpoints`, `generated_type_diff_summary`, `route_handler_touchpoints`, `test_failures`, and `owner_notes`. Core outputs should be evidence list, assumptions list, owner handoff, remediation order, and boundary warnings.

## skill_resource_bundle_review.md
Keep helper material synthetic and redacted. A minimal bundle can include a packet schema example, a review checklist, and a smoke fixture with two endpoints, one generated type mismatch, one route-handler note, and one failing test name. Do not encode project-specific endpoint names or private system identifiers. Any helper script must work only on synthetic or redacted packets.

## skill_boundary_review.md
Confirmed in-scope: redacted API contract drift packets, public-safe diffs, owner notes, and test-failure summaries. Out of scope: private incident detail, credentials, production logs, unredacted API specs, customer endpoint names, and runtime workspace files. The skill must clearly distinguish direct evidence from inference and should not promote assumptions into facts.

## skill_install_sync_request.md
Prepare an install handoff note that tells local operators how to mirror the tracked skill into the installed skill surface. The tracked package should request sync, name the target skill, and summarize the package contents, but it must not claim that sync was actually performed.

## skill_smoke_check.md
Smoke test on a tiny synthetic packet: two endpoints, one generated type mismatch, one route-handler touchpoint, and one failing test name. Expected result: the skill reports confirmed drift, labels uncertain items as assumptions, assigns owner handoff points, orders remediation from blocking contract mismatch to handler follow-up, and emits boundary warnings for any private data that must stay out of canon.

## skill_release_review.md
Release-ready only for the public-safe package boundary, not for private runtime data. The skill package is acceptable if it stays synthetic, avoids project-specific identifiers, and gives repeatable guidance for evidence-first review. The open risk is accidental drift from public-safe template language into private operational details, so the final review should recheck for that boundary before publishing.
